articles.title: On-Device Function Calling in Google AI Edge Gallery
articles.snippet: Google has introduced FunctionGemma, a specialized 270M parameter model designed to bring efficient, action-oriented AI experiences directly to mobile devices through on-device function calling. By leveraging Google AI Edge and LiteRT-LM, the model enables complex tasks—such as managing calendars, controlling device hardware, or executing specific game logic in the "Tiny Garden" demo—to be performed entirely offline with high speed and low latency. Available for testing in the Google AI Edge Gal
articles.ai_summary: Google launched FunctionGemma, a 270M parameter model for on-device function calling, enabling efficient AI tasks offline on mobile devices. This innovation enhances user experience by allowing complex operations like calendar management without internet dependency.
articles.why_it_matters: Revolutionizes mobile AI by enabling offline functionality for complex tasks.

articles.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
articles.snippet: Agent Development Kit (ADK) now supports a robust ecosystem of third-party tools and integrations. Connect your agents to GitHub, Notion, Hugging Face, and more to build capable, real-world applications.
articles.ai_summary: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now features a new ecosystem of third-party integrations, allowing developers to connect AI agents with tools like GitHub and Notion. This enhancement aims to facilitate the creation of more capable real-world applications.
articles.why_it_matters: Empowers developers to build more versatile AI applications through expanded integrations.

articles.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
articles.snippet: While keynotes are available online, Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas offers an irreplaceable in-person experience centered on networking, hands-on problem solving, and the transition to agentic AI. The event features specialized technical tracks covering everything from Gemini multimodal breakthroughs to zero-trust security on Cloud Run, providing developers with the tools to balance individual speed with organizational stability. Beyond formal sessions, the "in-person advantage" lies in over
articles.ai_summary: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem solving, featuring keynotes available online. The event emphasizes the transition to agentic AI and offers specialized tracks for developers.
articles.why_it_matters: Provides developers with essential tools and insights for navigating the evolving AI landscape.

articles.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
articles.snippet: Google has officially launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, which offers significantly faster GPU and NPU acceleration alongside seamless support for PyTorch and JAX. The update also introduces lower-precision data type support for increased efficiency and a commitment to more frequent security and dependency updates across the TensorFlow ecosystem. This transition solidifies LiteRT as Google's primary high-performance framework for deploying GenAI and advanced on-device inference.
articles.ai_summary: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, enhancing GPU and NPU acceleration and supporting PyTorch and JAX. This update positions LiteRT as the primary framework for deploying advanced AI and on-device inference.
articles.why_it_matters: Significantly boosts performance for AI applications, streamlining deployment processes.

articles.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
articles.snippet: Wednesday Build Hour is a weekly, interactive "technical gym session" led by Google Cloud experts to help developers and architects sharpen their cloud skills. Moving beyond passive slide decks, the program focuses on hands-on building, covering advanced topics like AI agents, Vertex AI, and developer productivity tools. Each hour-long session is designed to provide tangible results that participants can immediately deploy into their own workflows. It serves as a consistent, dedicated space for
articles.ai_summary: Wednesday Build Hour is a new weekly interactive session by Google Cloud experts aimed at helping developers enhance their cloud skills through hands-on experience. The program covers advanced AI topics and aims for immediate practical application.
articles.why_it_matters: Fosters a community of skilled developers ready to tackle complex cloud challenges.

articles.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
articles.snippet: The Gemini Code Assist team has introduced a suite of updates focused on streamlining the core coding workflow through high-velocity tools like Agent Mode with Auto Approve and Inline Diff Views. These enhancements, along with new features for precise context management and custom commands, aim to transform the AI from a general assistant into a highly tailored, seamless collaborator that adapts to your specific development style.
articles.ai_summary: The Gemini Code Assist team has rolled out updates to improve coding workflows with tools like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These enhancements aim to make AI a more tailored collaborator for developers.
articles.why_it_matters: Transforms AI into a more effective coding partner, enhancing developer productivity.

articles.snippet: Apple’s much-anticipated entry into the foldable phone market, the iPhone Fold, has the potential to redefine how you interact with mobile devices. By combining the portability of an iPhone with the expanded functionality of an iPad mini, this device could es…
articles.ai_summary: Apple's upcoming foldable phone, the iPhone Fold, is expected to reintroduce the fingerprint sensor, blending the features of an iPhone and an iPad mini. This could change user interaction with mobile devices.
articles.why_it_matters: Indicates a shift in mobile design strategies that could influence future device interactions.

articles.title: Apple OHTTP Relay: 14 Third-Party Endpoints, 6 Countries,Zero User Visibility
articles.snippet:
Posted by Joseph Goydish II via Fulldisclosure on Apr 02SUMMARY

Apple's Oblivious HTTP relay for Live Caller ID Lookup (iOS 18+) routes
traffic through 14 third-party endpoints across six countries. These include
an anonymous Delaware LLC sharing data with O…
articles.ai_summary: Apple's new OHTTP relay for Live Caller ID Lookup routes data through 14 third-party endpoints in six countries, raising concerns about user privacy and visibility.
articles.why_it_matters: Raises significant privacy concerns for users regarding data handling and transparency.

articles.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceX eyes over $2 trillion valuation in upcoming IPO
articles.snippet: Elon Musk's SpaceX is reportedly targeting an IPO valuation exceeding $2 trillion, potentially making it the largest stock market listing ever. The company is in discussions with prospective investors, aiming to raise up to $75 billion. This ambitious valuati…
articles.ai_summary: SpaceX is aiming for a valuation over $2 trillion in its upcoming IPO, potentially making it the largest stock market listing ever, with plans to raise $75 billion.
articles.why_it_matters: Could reshape the investment landscape and set new records in IPO history.

articles.title: Microsoftが音声生成モデル「MAI-Voice-1」・音声認識モデル「MAI-Transcribe-1」・画像生成モデル「MAI-Image-2」の3つのAI基盤モデルをリリース
articles.snippet: Microsoftが自社開発のAI基盤モデルとして、音声生成モデルの「MAI-Voice-1」、音声認識モデルの「MAI-Transcribe-1」、画像生成モデルの「MAI-Image-2」を発表しました。Today we're announcing 3 new world class MAI models, available in Foundry | Microsoft AIhttps://microsoft.ai/news/today-were-announcing-3-new-world-class-m…
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft has released three new AI models: MAI-Voice-1, MAI-Transcribe-1, and MAI-Image-2, enhancing its AI capabilities to compete with OpenAI and Google.
articles.why_it_matters: Strengthens Microsoft's position in the competitive AI landscape against major players.

articles.title: Microsoft executive touts Copilot sales traction as AI anxiety weighs on stock
articles.snippet: Analysts have said adoption of the $30-per-month Microsoft 365 Copilot is in the early stage.
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft's Copilot is seeing early adoption, but analysts express concerns about AI-related anxiety impacting stock performance.

articles.title: Microsoft Just Dropped 3 New AI Models to Take on OpenAI and Google
articles.snippet: Microsoft unveils MAI, its own AI models (Transcribe, Voice, Image) to rival OpenAI & Google. Available in Foundry, priced for scale, and integrated into Copilot.
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft's new MAI models are designed to rival offerings from OpenAI and Google, with scalable pricing and integration into the Copilot platform.

articles.title: Refactoring at the speed of mission: An "agent mesh" approach to legacy system modernization with Red Hat AI
articles.snippet: Legacy software doesn't retire itself. It sits in production, accumulating technical debt, resisting change, and quietly becoming a risk—not because of what it does, but because of what it can no longer support.That's the challenge facing leading systems inte…
articles.ai_summary: Red Hat AI discusses modernizing legacy systems through an 'agent mesh' approach, addressing the risks of outdated software in production.

articles.title: Unraveling Solo Ball's decline: How under-the-radar wrist injury has led to UConn guard's shooting woes
articles.snippet: The junior sharpshooter revealed how he's had to change his game amid a nagging injury.
articles.ai_summary: UConn guard Solo Ball's performance has declined due to a wrist injury, forcing him to adapt his playing style.

articles.title: Microsoft Teams: Annotations on Teams Single Window Sharing on macOS
articles.snippet: Presenters can now share a single application window and enable participants to annotate directly on top of that content, without exposing their entire desktop. This means cleaner presentations, protected privacy, and more engaging discussions across training sessions, workshops, and client meetings.GA date: May CY2026
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft Teams has introduced a feature allowing presenters to share a single application window with annotation capabilities, enhancing privacy and engagement.

articles.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Loss Prevention – Block External Domain/User action in DLP for SharePoint/OneDrive
articles.snippet: Block External Domain/User action in Microsoft Purview DLP for SharePoint and OneDrive, gives organizations stronger control over who can access sensitive files. With this enhancement, admins can configure DLP policies that block file access for specific external domains or individual users. This capability helps prevent unintended exposure by external users and supports enterprise compliance requirements.GA date: May CY2026Preview date: April CY2026
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft Purview now allows organizations to block external domain actions in DLP for SharePoint and OneDrive, enhancing data security and compliance.

articles.title: Microsoft Purview: Purview in Microsoft Admin Center
articles.snippet: AI and IT admins in Microsoft Admin Center can 1) gain visibility around oversharing risks and drive remediations 2) Understand how much of sensitive copilot interactions are protected and turn on Purview DLP for M365 Copilot right from there. Enables secure adoption of Copilot.GA date: September CY2026
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft Purview in the Admin Center provides insights into oversharing risks and enables secure adoption of Copilot, enhancing data governance.

articles.title: Microsoft Viva: Viva Glint and Viva Insights Integration - Exclusion List for data sharing
articles.snippet: Viva Glint admins will be able to manage an exclusion list in Viva Feature Access Management that prevents selected people and groups from being included in Viva Insights data shared with Viva Glint. This simplifies configuration and helps organizations apply consistent, privacy-conscious controls for the integration.GA date: May CY2026
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft Viva Glint now allows admins to create an exclusion list for data sharing with Viva Insights, enhancing privacy controls for organizations. This feature aims to simplify configuration and ensure consistent data management.
articles.why_it_matters: Enhances data privacy management in workplace tools.

articles.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Loss Prevention – Inline DLP for Prompts for Microsoft Foundry apps and agents
articles.snippet: With Purview enabled in Microsoft Foundry, Purview admins can setup inline DLP policies to prompts on Foundry built apps and agents to ensure data loss prevention of sensitive data.  GA date: June CY2026Preview date: May CY2026
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft Purview introduces inline data loss prevention (DLP) policies for prompts in Foundry apps, helping protect sensitive data. This feature will be generally available by June 2026, with a preview in May.
articles.why_it_matters: Strengthens data security in AI application development.

articles.title: Sources detail Fidji Simo's moves at OpenAI, including spearheading the TBPN acquisition and pushing OpenAI to cut Sora and avoid other social media products (Stephanie Palazzolo/The Information)
articles.snippet:

articles.title: Nobody Could Answer the CFO’s Question on the ROI from AI Adoption; TechWish Built a Platform That Can
articles.snippet: Deploying AI is not the challenge, it’s how to connect its usage patterns to measurable business outcomes. That’s the gap TechWish addresses. Vishnu Reddy...
articles.ai_summary: TechWish has developed a platform to connect AI usage patterns with measurable business outcomes, addressing the challenge of demonstrating ROI from AI adoption. This could help businesses justify their AI investments.
articles.why_it_matters: Provides a solution for businesses struggling to measure AI effectiveness.

articles.title: lmux-azure-foundry 0.3.0
articles.snippet: Azure AI Foundry provider for lmux

articles.title: Women's March Madness Final Four AI predictions pick UConn over field
articles.snippet: Here's which women's Final Four team AI predicts to win the NCAA Tournament among South Carolina, UCLA, Texas and UConn.
articles.ai_summary: AI predictions favor UConn to win the Women's NCAA Tournament Final Four, showcasing the growing role of AI in sports analytics and predictions.

articles.title: Even Microsoft knows Copilot shouldn't be trusted with anything important
articles.snippet: Terms admit it is for entertainment only and may get things wrong A recent surge of interest in Microsoft's Terms of Use for Copilot is a reminder that AI helpers are really just a bit of fun.…
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft's Copilot is being highlighted for its limitations, as its terms of use clarify that it should not be relied upon for critical tasks. This serves as a reminder of the current capabilities and risks of AI tools.
articles.why_it_matters: Cautions users about over-reliance on AI for important decisions.

articles.title: Cookie-controlled PHP webshells: A stealthy tradecraft in Linux hosting environments
articles.snippet:
Cookie-gated PHP webshells use obfuscation, php-fpm execution, and cron-based persistence to evade detection in Linux hosting environments. This post examines how this tradecraft conceals execution behind specially crafted HTTP cookies.
The post Cookie-contro…
articles.ai_summary: The article discusses the use of cookie-controlled PHP webshells in Linux hosting environments, detailing how they evade detection through obfuscation and cron-based persistence. This highlights a sophisticated method of maintaining stealth in cyber operations.
articles.why_it_matters: Sheds light on advanced cyber threats in web hosting.

articles.title: Microsoft wants Edge to automatically open by default every time you turn on your Windows 11 PC
articles.snippet: A new update now in testing sets Edge to automatically appear at startup, and you are forced to opt-out if you don't want it.
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft is testing a new update that sets Edge to open automatically at startup on Windows 11 PCs, requiring users to opt-out if they prefer not to have it launch. This move aims to increase Edge's usage.
articles.why_it_matters: Indicates aggressive strategies to boost browser adoption.
